text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When developers review each other's code, they can catch bugs early, before the code reaches production. This saves time and reduces the cost of fixing errors later. Beyond finding mistakes, code reviews promote knowledge sharing within the team. Team members learn from different coding styles, techniques, and solutions. This helps spread expertise and reduces reliance on single individuals. Ultimately, regular code reviews improve overall team quality. They encourage cleaner, more maintainable code and foster a culture of collaboration and continuous learning. A team that reviews code well builds better software together.
